## đź—‚ď¸Ź Pulse â€” Sheet Operations  (Workflow 0)

Central data layer for **all Google Sheets read/write operations**. Every other Pulse workflow calls this webhook â€” never writes to the Sheet directly. This keeps all column-mapping logic in one place.

**Webhook path:** `pulse-sheet-ops`  |  **Method:** POST  |  Two response types: JSON data (reads) Â· `{ success: true }` (writes)

**Supported actions:** `GET_ALL_POSTS` Â· `GET_POST_BY_ID` Â· `UPDATE_PLATFORM_VARIANT` Â· `UPDATE_MULTIPLE_PLATFORMS` Â· `WRITE_CONTENT_PROMPTS` Â· `WRITE_IMAGE_PROMPTS` Â· `UPDATE_STATUS` Â· `UPDATE_ANALYTICS` Â· `UPDATE_FIRST_COMMENT`

## âš™ď¸Ź Setup â€” Do This First

**Step 1 â€” Google Sheets credential**
Open each `GSheets` node â†’ Credential â†’ select (or create) your **Google Sheets OAuth2** account.

**Step 2 â€” Spreadsheet ID**
In both GSheets nodes replace `YOUR_SPREADSHEET_ID` with the ID from your Sheet URL:
`docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/`**`PASTE_ID_HERE`**`/edit`

**Step 3 â€” Sheet tab name**
Change `Sheet1` to your actual tab name (default is `Sheet1`).

**Step 4 â€” Row 1 headers**
Row 1 of your Sheet must contain all **94 column headers** in exact order. See `README.md` for the full comma-separated list.

**Step 5 â€” Activate**
Toggle the workflow ON after setup.

## đź“– READ path
Triggers: `GET_ALL_POSTS` and `GET_POST_BY_ID`

Reads **all rows** from the Sheet, then the Code node:
- Converts `snake_case` columns â†’ `camelCase` app fields
- Splits `twitter_hashtags` (CSV string) â†’ `string[]`
- Casts analytics strings â†’ `number | null`
- Filters by `statusFilter` / `platformFilter` / date range *(GET_ALL_POSTS)*
- Finds single row by `post_id` *(GET_POST_BY_ID)*

## âśŹď¸Ź WRITE path
Triggers: all 7 `UPDATE_*` / `WRITE_*` actions

The **Prepare Write Row** Code node:
- Reads `action` + `payload` from the webhook body
- Builds a flat row object with **only** the columns to change
- Always includes `post_id` so the GSheets Update node can find the right row

The GSheets **Update** node matches on `post_id` and overwrites only the supplied columns. **No new rows are ever created.**

Hashtag arrays are joined to CSV before writing (e.g. `['ai','build']` â†’ `'ai,build'`).

## đź”‘ Column mapping reference

All 94 columns follow the pattern:
`{platform}_{field}` e.g. `twitter_text`, `instagram_status`, `facebook_engagement_rate`

**Platforms:** `twitter` Â· `threads` Â· `instagram` Â· `facebook` Â· `skool`

**Per-platform fields (Ă—5 platforms = 45 cols):**
`_text` Â· `_content_prompt` Â· `_image_prompt` Â· `_image_url` Â· `_hashtags` Â· `_status` Â· `_generated_at` Â· `_scheduled_at` Â· `_published_at`

**Analytics (Ă—5 = 35 cols):**
`_platform_post_id` Â· `_impressions` Â· `_likes` Â· `_comments` Â· `_shares` Â· `_engagement_rate` Â· `_fetched_at`

**First comment (Ă—5 = 10 cols):**
`_first_comment` Â· `_first_comment_status`

**Plus 4 base cols:** `post_id` Â· `linkedin_text` Â· `linkedin_image_url` Â· `posted_at`
